references.text: Johns, RJ. 1988. The flowering plants of Papuasia, Dicotyledons 2: 121-125.
Johnson, LAS. 1982. Notes on Casuarinaceae. II. Journal of the Adelaide Botanic Garden 6: 73-87
Johnson, LAS. 1988. Notes on Casuarinaceae III: The new genus Ceuthostoma. Telopea 3: 133-137.

Torrey, JG & RH Berg. 1988. Some morphological features for generic characterization among the Casuarinaceae. American Journal of Botany 75(6): 864-875

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 10 May 2026

species.citation: L. , Amoen. Acad. 4 (1759) 143 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 169; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 1; Romeroso et al., Asian J. Biol. Life Sc. 10 (2021) 437. ssp. equisetifolia. Distribution: LUMBUCAN, LUZON: Abra, Albay, Cagayan, Camarines, Ilocos Norte, Isabela, Laguna, Pangasinan, Quezon, Sorsogon, Tarlac, Zambales, MINDANAO: Surigao, PALAUI. Notes: Throughout the Philippines along sandy seashores, extending inland in open sandy valleys along streams. Usually gregarious. 0-800m. Native.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Australia, Bangladesh, Borneo, Cambodia, India, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, New Caledonia, New Guinea, Nicobar Isls, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. ALABAT, BORACAY (photos), CEBU, HOMONHON, LUMBUCAN, LUZON: Abra, Albay, Aurora, Benguet, Cagayan, Camarines, Camarines Sur, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, Laguna, Nueva Ecija, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Tarlac, Zambales, MANGSEE, MINASAWA, MINDANAO: Misamis, Surigao, Zamboanga del Sur, MINDORO, PALAUI, PALAWAN, POLILLO, SAMAR, SIBUYAN, TABLAS.
species.notes: Also referred to as Casuarina litorea L. Pantropic in cultivation. 0-800m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 18 August 2017

species.citation: L.A.S.Johnson , Telopea 3 (1988) 137; Type: Merrill 785 (A, holo), Palawan: Ewiig (Iwahig) River.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. MINDANAO: Davao Oriental, PALAWAN.
Borneo, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines. PALAWAN.
species.notes: Primary and secondary forest on ultramafic soils. 0-1525m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 15 January 2023

species.citation: (Whitmore) L.A.S.Johnson , J. Adelaide Bot. Gard. 6 (1982) 84; ≡ Casuarina nobilis Whitmore , Trop. Rain Forests Far East (1975) 134.

(Miq.) L.A.S.Johnson , J. Adelaide Bot. Gard. 6 (1982) 85 ; Fernando et al., Forest Flora of Dinagat (2017) 51; Coritico et al., PJS 151 (2022) 2438; Villanueva et al., Biodiversitas 23 (2022) 6496; ≡ Casuarina rumphiana Miq. , Flora 48 (1865) 23 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 1.
species.distribution: Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi. BILIRAN, BUCAS GRANDE, DINAGAT, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Aurora, Camarines Norte, Camarines Sur, Laguna, NCR, Quezon, Sorsogon, Tarlac, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao, Davao del Sur, Lanao, Lanao del Sur, Misamis Occidental, Zamboanga, MINDORO, PALAWAN, PANAY, SAMAR, SIBUYAN.
species.notes: N Luzon to Palawan and Mindanao. In montane forests, often gregarious. 100-1000m.

species.citation: (Jungh. ex de Vriese) L.A.S.Johnson , J. Adelaide Bot. Gard. 6 (1982) 86; ≡ Casuarina sumatrana Jungh. ex de Vriese , Tijdschr. Natuurl. Gesch. Physiol. 11 (1844) 115 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 1.
species.distribution: Borneo, Java, Myanmar to Sumatra, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi. BOHOL, LEYTE, LUZON: Laguna, Sorsogon, MINDANAO, PALAWAN, PANAY.
